mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand driving corporate earnings, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as earnings revisions increase.
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of months or rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE more than 0.3 percentage points over three months) signals labor market weakness that erodes consumer spending and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If instances triggered by UNRATE spikes do not produce negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases or average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 2% and consumer spending (RSAFS) is growing month-over-month, accommodative monetary policy amplifies the positive demand impulse, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days than either signal alone.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with demand growth
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields negative average 20-day returns or if the interaction term in a regression is not statistically significant (p<0.10) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During the late-cycle expansion phase when industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates from above 5% year-over-year to below 2%, it signals capacity constraints and an impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (INDPRO y/y growth decelerating from >5% to <2%)
failure_condition: If INDPRO deceleration episodes in late-cycle do not precede negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases or the signal's Sharpe ratio is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labor market that supports rising disposable income and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If monthly payroll growth does not predict positive 5-day returns in at least 55% of months or rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate (more than 25 basis points in one month) signals tightening monetary policy that raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If months with >0.25pp FEDFUNDS hikes do not show negative average 20-day returns or hit rate <55% over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is growing month-over-month and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 2%, the combination of supply expansion and accommodative monetary conditions creates a positive environment for equities, boosting S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: supply expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If the joint condition yields negative average 60-day returns or if the interaction term in a regression is not significant (p<0.10) over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During the early recovery phase when housing starts (HOUST) climb from cycle troughs below 1 million units, rising residential investment signals a self-reinforcing economic recovery that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
regime_hint: early recovery (HOUST climbing from trough below 1M units)
failure_condition: If HOUST recovery episodes (rising from <1M) do not produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases or average return is not significantly positive.
